title: One-time `migrate-records` command: split all issue partitions, retrofit sprints, then enable the guard on the dogfood Instance
description: Build a one-time `to-execution migrate-records` command (distinct from `migrate`, which stays location-only per ADR-0008): split `backlog.json` and every `sprint-<N>/sprint-<N>-issues.json` into per-file `<uuid>-<slug>.json` records (preserving — grandfathering — existing `EXEC-NNN` ids), and retrofit every `sprint_<N>.json` to canonical-sentinel form. All writes go through the helper (guard-safe). Then dogfood it on the root Instance on a clean tree and enable the channel-guard feature flag here.
acceptance_criteria: `migrate-records` splits `backlog.json` AND each sprint issue-companion file into per-file records, preserving legacy ids
Every `sprint_<N>.json` is retrofitted to canonical-sentinel form via the helper
Repo root is resolved by absolute path, never an inherited cwd — sprint-4 postmortem
Every written record re-reads schema-valid BEFORE any monolith is deleted — verify-before-destroy, postmortem
Idempotent: a second run is a no-op; it never clobbers an existing per-file record — sprint-6 restamp lesson
Inputs are committed first; recovery is a documented `git checkout` — postmortem
Dogfood run is sequenced by the Team Lead on a clean tree with no WIP lanes; verification Bash uses no `&&` chain that could trigger a file-reverting linter — sprint-8 lesson
After migration completes and CLI adoption (EXEC-101) is in place, the channel-guard flag is enabled on this Instance
